text:
AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY - ACADEMIC CALENDAR, TERM I, ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-27
Prepared By: Dr. Satyajit Pangaonkar (Asso. Dean Academics)
Endorsed By: Dr. Manesh Patil (Registrar), Dr. Surendra Rahamatkar (Pro. Vice Chancellor)
Approved By: Dr. Nitin Rane (Vice Chancellor). Date: 11th May 2026.

IMPORTANT NOTE - MAHAKAL SAVARI
From 13 July 2026 to 24 August 2026, all Mondays are holidays and Saturdays are working days due to Mahakal Savari procession in Ujjain.

COMMENCEMENT OF ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-27
The academic year 2026-27 commences on 06 July 2026 for all programmes at Avantika University.

ORIENTATION DATES - TERM I AY 2026-27
FY BDes, FY MDes, FY EMBA orientation: 29 June 2026 to 03 July 2026.
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY MBA, FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM orientation: 27 July 2026 to 31 July 2026.
PhD (School of Design, School of Engineering, School of Management, School of Law) orientation: 17 August 2026.

STUDENT REGISTRATION DATES - TERM I AY 2026-27
BDes, SY MDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BSc(A), SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA, SY EMBA registration: 06 July 2026.
TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA registration: 22 June 2026.
BTech (Honours), BCA (Honours), SY MCA, SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth Year BALLB, Forth Year BBALLB registration: 13 July 2026.
First Day of Reporting is the Registration Day for all students.

PUBLIC HOLIDAYS IN TERM I AY 2026-27
Independence Day: 15 August 2026.
Raksha Bandhan: 28 August 2026.
Avantika University Foundation Day: 11 September 2026.
Ganesh Chaturthi: 14 September 2026.
Gandhi Jayanti: 02 October 2026.
Vijaya Dashami (Dussehra): 20 October 2026.
Diwali Break: 06 November 2026 to 13 November 2026.
Christmas: 25 December 2026.
Convocation: 23 January 2027.

. ACADEMIC SESSION TERM I - START AND END DATES FY BDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, FY MDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BSc(A), SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA, FY EMBA, SY EMBA: Term I from 06 July 2026 to 20 November 2026. BDes, SY MDes: Term I from 06 July 2026 to 05 November 2026. BTech Honours, BCA Honours, SY MCA, SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB: Term I from 13 July 2026 to 27 November 2026. TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA: Term I from 22 June 2026 to 05 November 2026

FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM: Term I from 03 August 2026 to 18 December 2026. PhD (all schools): Term I from 17 August 2026 to 31 December 2026. Students get 3 days Preparatory Leave after the end of the academic term.

. JURY AND PRACTICAL EXAMINATION DATES - TERM I AY 2026-27 FY BDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, FY MDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BSc(A), SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA, FY EMBA, SY EMBA jury: 23 November 2026 to 27 November 2026. BTech Honours, BCA Honours, SY MCA, SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB jury: 01 December 2026 to 04 December 2026. BDes, SY MDes, TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA jury: 18 November 2026 to 20 November 2026
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM jury: 21 December 2026 to 24 December 2026.

. END-TERM EXAMINATIONS - TERM I AY 2026-27 SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA, TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA exams: After course completion, next immediate Monday. SY BSc(A), BCA Honours, SY MCA, FY EMBA, SY EMBA exams: 07 December 2026 to 10 December 2026. SY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB exams: 04 December 2026 to 11 December 2026. FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM exams: 24 December 2026 to 01 January 2027
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM exams: 28 December 2026 to 31 December 2026.

ANNOUNCEMENT OF RESULTS - TERM I AY 2026-27
FY BDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, FY MDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BSc(A), SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA, FY EMBA, SY EMBA results: 15 December 2026.
BDes, BTech Honours, SY MDes, BCA Honours, SY MCA, SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB results: 24 December 2026.
TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA results: 10 December 2026.
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM results: 15 January 2027.

. SEMESTER BREAK AFTER TERM I AY 2026-27 FY BDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, FY MDes, SY MDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA semester break: 16 November 2026 to 20 November 2026. BTech Honours, BCA Honours semester break: 05 December 2026 to 06 December 2026. BDes, TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA, FY EMBA, SY EMBA semester break: 23 November 2026 to 29 November 2026. SY BSc(A), SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB semester break: 11 December 2026 to 13 December 2026
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB semester break: 01 January 2027 to 03 January 2027.

. COMMENCEMENT OF TERM II AY 2026-27 FY BDes, SY BDes, TY BDes, BDes, FY MDes, SY MDes, SY BTech, TY BTech, SY BCA, TY BCA, SY BSc(A), SY BBA, SY BCom, SY IMBA Term II starts: 30 November 2026. BTech Honours, BCA Honours Term II starts: 07 December 2026. TY BBA, TY IMBA, TY BCom, SY MBA Term II starts: 01 December 2026. SY BALLB, TY BALLB, SY BBALLB, TY BBALLB, Forth BALLB, Forth BBALLB, FY EMBA, SY EMBA Term II starts: 14 December 2026
FY BTech, FY BCA, FY BBA, FY BCom, FY IMBA, FY MBA, FY BSc(A), FY BSc(CP), FY MCA, FY BALLB, FY BBALLB, FY LLM Term II starts: 04 January 2027.

. ABBREVIATIONS FOR AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY PROGRAMMES FY = First Year, SY = Second Year, TY = Third Year. BTech = Bachelor of Technology, BCA = Bachelor of Computer Applications. BBA = Bachelor of Business Administration, BCom = Bachelor of Commerce. BDes = Bachelor of Design, MDes = Master of Design. MBA = Master of Business Administration, MCA = Master of Computer Applications. IMBA = Integrated MBA, EMBA = Executive MBA. BALLB = BA LLB (integrated law), BBALLB = BBA LLB (integrated law), LLM = Master of Laws

BSc(A) = Bachelor of Science in Animation, BSc(CP) = Bachelor of Science in Computer Programs. BCA(H) = BCA Honours, BTech(H) = BTech Honours. SoD = School of Design, SoE = School of Engineering, SoM = School of Management, SoLPP = School of Law Policy and Planning.

AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY - FACULTY & KEY FUNCTIONARIES
Source: www.avantikauniversity.edu.in
Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh | An MIT Group Institution

Founder:
- Prof. Vishwanath D. Karad | Founder, Avantika University
  Role: Established Avantika University as India's first design-centered institution. Initiated formation of the first nine private engineering colleges in Maharashtra. Serves as Chief Patron of MAEER's MIT Group of Institutions.

Executive President:
- Sunil Karad | Executive President, Avantika University
  Role: Managing growth and long-term success of educational establishments within MAEER's MIT Group, Pune. Oversees development of institutions serving 50,000+ learners annually. Trustee and Treasurer of MAEER's MIT Group. Founded Edugild — India's global education technology accelerator. Established 10+ new-age educational institutions.

Chancellor:
- Prof. Sanjay Govind Dhande | Chancellor, Avantika University
  Role: Oversees the university's strategic direction. Former Director of IIT Kanpur. Member of Scientific Advisory Council to the Prime Minister of India. Former member of TRAI (Telecom Regulatory Authority of India). Expert in Computer Aided Design, Rapid Prototyping, and Reverse Engineering. Founded PDPM Indian Institute of Information Technology Design and Manufacturing, Jabalpur.

Vice Chancellor:
- Dr. Nitin Rane | Vice Chancellor, Avantika University
  Role: Leads university academic and administrative operations. Expertise in Process Design, Energy Efficiency, Environmental Pollution Control, Thermodynamics, and Equipment Design. Has played a key role in setting up many institutes and universities for MIT Group.

Pro Vice Chancellor:
- Prof. Surendra N. Rahamatkar | Pro Vice Chancellor & Dean (School of Engineering)
  Role: Oversees institution building, academic administration, strategic planning, global and national rankings, accreditations, research and development. Editor-in-Chief of International Journal on Computer Science Engineering Trends. Mentor of Change for Atal Tinkering Labs under NITI Aayog. Collaborates with Oracle, IBM, Infosys, TCS, Microsoft, and Google India.

Registrar:
- Dr. Manesh Patil | Registrar, Avantika University
  Role: Institutional leadership and academic administration. Over 22 years of experience in higher education. Expertise in chemical engineering and sustainable design. Recipient of Best Teacher of the Year award (2013).

AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY - ACADEMIC ORDINANCES
Programs: B.Tech, B.Des, MBA, M.Des, M.Tech, MCA
Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh | An MIT Group Institution
(Under NEP 2020)

Program Overview:
- Duration: 4 years / 8 semesters
- Full-time degree program in Engineering & Technology
- Under NEP 2020, AICTE and State Government of Madhya Pradesh guidelines
- Medium of instruction: English (other languages may be used as per regulatory requirements)

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum 45% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) in 10+2 (or equivalent)
- As per AICTE / State Government of Madhya Pradesh / UGC guidelines
- The minimum qualification is decided by the respective Board of Studies and approved by Academic Council of the University

Examination Structure:
- First Year: First Semester Exam + Second Semester Exam
- Second Year: Third Semester Exam + Fourth Semester Exam
- Third Year: Fifth Semester Exam + Sixth Semester Exam
- Fourth Year: Seventh Semester Exam + Eighth Semester Exam

Assessment Components:
- CCE (Continuous Comprehensive Evaluation): assignments, quizzes, practicals, lab work, class participation
- End Semester Examination: time-bound written exams
- Practical / Lab examinations
- Major Project (final year)
- Compulsory Internship/Industrial Training (third or fourth year)

Internship:
- Internship is compulsory as part of the B.Tech program
- The Major Project pertains to the internship, jointly assessed by the University and the host organization
- Decision on equivalent courses for internship taken by the respective Board of Studies approved by Academic Council

ATKT (Allowed to Keep Terms) Rules:
- A student is allowed to appear in next year/semester examinations even with failed subjects (backlogs), subject to prescribed limits
- Maximum number of backlog subjects to continue to the next year/semester is specified in Academic Regulations
- A student cannot proceed to the next higher year if backlog count exceeds the prescribed limit
- Students must clear all backlogs within the maximum program duration

Maximum Program Duration:
- B.Tech program must be completed within 8 years (double the 4-year normal duration)
- Students who cannot complete within this duration may be considered for re-admission as per university policy

GRADING SYSTEM (Choice Based Credit System — applicable to all programs):

Grade | % Marks Range | Grade Points | Description
A+    | 90 – 100      | 10           | Outstanding
A     | 81 – 90       | 9            | Excellent
B+    | 71 – 80       | 8            | Very Good
B     | 61 – 70       | 7            | Good
C     | 51 – 60       | 6            | Average
D     | 41 – 50       | 5            | Satisfactory
F     | Below 41      | 0            | Fail
FR    | —             | 0            | Fail due to Ragging
W     | —             | 0            | Withdrawn

Note: FR grade is awarded when a student is found guilty of ragging. W grade is awarded on withdrawal from a course.

SGPA (Semester Grade Point Average):
SGPA = Σ(Ci × Gi) / ΣCi
where Ci = credit of i-th subject in that semester, Gi = grade point obtained in i-th subject

CGPA (Cumulative Grade Point Average):
CGPA = Σ(Ci × Si) / ΣCi
where Si = SGPA of i-th semester, Ci = total credits in that semester

Percentage Conversion:
Percentage = CGPA × 10

CGPA to Division (Award of Degree — B.Tech):
CGPA Score           | Division
8.5 and above        | First Class with Distinction
6.5 to below 8.5     | First Class
5.5 to below 6.5     | Second Class
4.5 to below 5.5     | Pass Class
Below 4.5            | Fail

Award of B.Tech Degree:
- Must pass all 8 semesters and all prescribed subjects/courses
- Must complete compulsory internship
- Must fulfil credit requirements as prescribed by Board of Studies
- No outstanding dues to the University
- No pending disciplinary proceedings

Promotion to Higher Year (B.Tech):
- First to Second Year: may be promoted with backlogs not exceeding prescribed limit
- Second to Third Year: may be promoted with backlogs not exceeding prescribed limit
- Third to Fourth Year: may be promoted with backlogs not exceeding prescribed limit
- Academic probation applies to students who exceed backlog limits

Re-admission:
- Candidates unable to complete program within stipulated time may be considered for re-admission as per university policy
- Subject to Academic Council approval

Program Overview:
- Duration: 4 years / 8 semesters
- Full-time degree program in Design
- Under NEP 2020 and relevant regulatory body (AICTE/UGC)
- Studio-based and project-based pedagogy

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum qualification as per provisions of the relevant Regulatory Body, AICTE, UGC and University guidelines
- Portfolio/aptitude test may be required as part of admissions process
- As per State Government of Madhya Pradesh guidelines

Examination Structure:
- Same semester-wise structure as B.Tech (8 semesters over 4 years)
- Jury-based assessment panels for design studio work (internal + external experts)
- Rubric-based evaluation using standardised assessment sheets
- Theory subjects may have written examinations
- Major Design Project in final year

Assessment:
- CCE through studio reviews, critiques, assignments, participation
- Jury Assessment: formal end-of-semester panels evaluate projects
- Venue-wise lists displayed at least 3 days before Jury

Grading, SGPA, CGPA: Same grading system as B.Tech (A+ to F/FR/W)

CGPA to Division (B.Des):
Same criteria as B.Tech (8.5+ Distinction, 6.5+ First Class, 5.5+ Second Class, 4.5+ Pass)

ATKT Rules, Maximum Duration, Award of Degree: Same framework as B.Tech

Program Overview:
- Duration: 2 years / 4 semesters (Post Graduate)
- Advanced design education with research component
- Dissertation/Thesis in final semester

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum qualification: Bachelor's degree in Design, Architecture, Fine Arts, Engineering, or equivalent as per regulatory requirements
- Entrance exam / portfolio / interview as per university requirements

Examination Structure:
- First Semester M.Des Exam
- Second Semester M.Des Exam
- Third Semester M.Des Exam
- Fourth Semester M.Des Exam (includes Dissertation/Thesis evaluation)

Assessment:
- CCE through studio work, seminars, presentations, research progress
- Jury and panel-based evaluation for design work
- Dissertation defence/viva voce in final semester

Grading: Same Choice Based Credit System as undergraduate programs (A+ to F/FR/W)

CGPA to Division (M.Des):
Same CGPA-based criteria as undergraduate programs
Maximum Duration: M.Des must be completed within 4 years (double the 2-year normal duration)
ORDINANCE NO. 29: MASTER OF BUSINESS ADMINISTRATION (MBA)

Program Overview:
- Duration: 2 years / 4 semesters (Post Graduate)
- Management education program
- Under AICTE/UGC guidelines

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum qualification: Bachelor's degree with required percentage as per AICTE/UGC norms
- Entrance exam (CAT/MAT/CMAT/university entrance) may be required
- As per provisions of the relevant Regulatory Body duly approved by Academic Council

Examination Structure:
- First Semester MBA Exam
- Second Semester MBA Exam
- Summer Internship (between Year 1 and Year 2)
- Third Semester MBA Exam
- Fourth Semester MBA Exam (includes MBA Project/Dissertation)

Assessment:
- CCE through case studies, assignments, presentations, group projects
- End semester written examinations
- Summer internship project report
- Final MBA Project/Dissertation

Grading: Same Choice Based Credit System (A+ to F/FR/W)

CGPA to Division (MBA): Same CGPA criteria as other programs
Maximum Duration: MBA must be completed within 4 years

Program Overview:
- Duration: 2 years / 4 semesters (Post Graduate)
- Advanced engineering/technology specialization
- Under AICTE/UGC guidelines (NEP 2020)

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum qualification: B.Tech/B.E. or equivalent degree in relevant branch with required percentage
- In accordance with Rollout Directions of the relevant Regulatory Body (AICTE/UGC)
- As per provisions of the State Government of Madhya Pradesh
- The Admission Policy, eligibility & Selection Procedure may be reviewed and amended as per norms and guidelines of UGC from time to time, subject to approval of Academic Council

Examination Structure:
- First Semester M.Tech Exam
- Second Semester M.Tech Exam
- Third Semester M.Tech Exam
- Fourth Semester M.Tech Exam (includes M.Tech Thesis/Dissertation)

Assessment:
- CCE through assignments, lab work, seminars, research progress
- End semester examinations
- Thesis/Dissertation: research work, publication (if applicable), viva voce

Grading: Same Choice Based Credit System (A+ to F/FR/W)
CGPA to Division: Same criteria as other programs
Maximum Duration: M.Tech must be completed within 4 years
ORDINANCE NO. 31: MASTER OF COMPUTER APPLICATIONS (MCA)

Program Overview:
- Duration: 2 years / 4 semesters (Post Graduate)
- Under UGC/AICTE guidelines
- Computer Applications with programming, software development, and project work

Admission Eligibility:
- Minimum qualification: Bachelor's degree with Mathematics as a subject (B.Sc./BCA/B.Tech or equivalent)
- As per UGC/AICTE norms and university guidelines

Examination Structure:
- First Semester MCA Exam
- Second Semester MCA Exam
- Third Semester MCA Exam
- Fourth Semester MCA Exam (includes MCA Project)

Assessment:
- CCE through programming assignments, lab work, mini-projects
- End semester written and practical examinations
- Major project in final semester with viva voce

Grading: Same Choice Based Credit System (A+ to F/FR/W)
CGPA to Division: Same criteria
Maximum Duration: MCA must be completed within 4 years

Credit Mapping and Transfer:
- Subjects/courses carry credits as prescribed by Board of Studies and approved by Academic Council
- MOOC/online course credit transfer applicable as per NEP 2020 guidelines (specific credits as prescribed)
- Add-on courses may contribute to credit count as per regulations

Academic Integrity:
- UFM (Unfair Means) provisions apply to all programs
- Ragging results in FR grade (Fail due to Ragging) for that semester
- Academic misconduct handled by Disciplinary/UFM Committee

Re-examination Policy:
- Supplementary/Backlog examinations available for failed subjects
- Makeup examinations for students with approved absence
- Improvement examinations to improve grades (original CCE carried forward)
- Application process: School Dean signs, fees paid, application submitted to Exam Office

Attendance Requirements:
- Minimum attendance as per University regulations required to appear in examinations
- Students below minimum attendance threshold may be detained from appearing in exams
- Medical/special leave may be considered with proper documentation

Grading Remarks:
- Incomplete (I): awarded when CCE/work is not completed; student must complete within next semester
- W (Withdrawn): student withdraws from course before completion; zero grade points
- FR (Fail due to Ragging): awarded on proven ragging; zero grade points; appears on transcript

Transcript:
- Official transcripts issued by Controller of Examinations (CoE)
- Grades for each course, SGPA for each semester, and CGPA listed
- Percentage equivalent (CGPA × 10) stated as per CoE notification
- Transcript requested through Student Office with No-Dues clearance

Award of Degree — Common Conditions:
1. Passed all prescribed courses/subjects in all semesters
2. Completed compulsory internship/project as required by program
3. Fulfilled minimum credit requirements as prescribed
4. No outstanding fee dues to the University
5. No pending disciplinary proceedings
6. Fulfilled attendance requirements

Division is awarded only after completion of the full program, based on overall CGPA.

Governing Authority:
- Academic Council of Avantika University is the supreme academic body
- Board of Studies (per School/Department) prescribes curriculum, credits, evaluation schemes
- Controller of Examinations (CoE) administers examinations and results
- Vice Chancellor approves final decisions on degree awards, special cases
- UGC/AICTE regulations and NEP 2020 framework govern overall academic structure

Programs Offered at Avantika University:
Undergraduate (4-year):
- B.Tech (Bachelor of Technology) — Ordinance 26
- B.Des (Bachelor of Design) — Ordinance 27

Postgraduate (2-year):
- M.Des (Master of Design) — Ordinance 28
- MBA (Master of Business Administration) — Ordinance 29
- M.Tech (Master of Technology) — Ordinance 30
- MCA (Master of Computer Applications) — Ordinance 31

AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY - STUDENT HANDBOOK
Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh | An MIT Group Institution
Academic Year 2025-26

Welcome to Avantika University. This handbook brings together the policies and procedures that most directly affect student life — from enrolment and examinations to hostel living, health services, transport and the support available to students. It has been compiled from the University's official Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).

This handbook is a reader-friendly summary. Where any question of interpretation arises, the University's approved SOPs, the Academic Regulations, and UGC norms shall prevail. Procedures, fees and timelines are reviewed periodically; always confirm the current position with the relevant office, the ERP portal, or official notice boards.

First point of contact: For most day-to-day matters the relevant office (Student Office, Exam Office, Hostel Warden, Health Centre, Transport Office, or Faculty Mentor) is the first point of contact. A consolidated list of helplines and key contacts is published on the University website, ERP portal and notice boards at the start of each academic year.

Once admitted, the Student Office manages enrolment and registration. The process confirms records, verifies documents, and sets up digital access before classes begin.

Before Arrival:
Admitted students receive an email on their registered email ID with the enrolment form link, document checklist, academic calendar, reporting date and orientation schedule. These communications are sent at least 10 days before the reporting date. Complete the enrolment form and keep listed documents and study materials ready before travelling to campus.

. Reporting and Document Verification: A student reporting desk is set up on the reporting date with dedicated staff to guide students through the process. Document verification is completed on the day of reporting (or within 3 working days if arriving late). A checklist of required original documents is used for every student, and an acknowledgement report is generated listing documents submitted and retained, signed by the student and verifying officer
Hostel residents are identified with the help of the Wardens, who submit the reporting list on the reporting day.

Registration, Email ID and LMS:
Course registration is completed within 5 working days of reporting. If unable to register, the Student Office will assist within 24 hours of reporting the issue. University email ID and LMS account are created by the ICT team / LMS administrator, normally within 3 working days of the confirmed list.

Key definitions:
- SIS (Student Information System): holds academic records and course registration
- LMS (Learning Management System): where students access course content, assignments and announcements
- ERP Portal: the University's system used to publish results and notifications
Assessment at Avantika University combines continuous internal evaluation with end-of-semester assessment. The Exam Office, headed by the Controller of Examinations (CoE), administers the process.

CCE (Continuous & Comprehensive Evaluation):
CCE is the ongoing internal assessment carried out through the semester — assignments, LMS activities, quizzes, class participation and studio/lab work. CCE marks are recorded by course faculty and entered in the University's systems. Faculty submit CCE marks within the deadlines set in the Examination Calendar (typically within one to two weeks of a course's completion).

Jury Assessment (Project/Applied Work):
A Jury is a formal end-of-semester assessment panel of internal and external experts who evaluate project work against defined rubrics. The School of Design follows Jury-based assessment for its courses, and other Schools use Juries for project, field and applied work. Venue-wise student lists are displayed on notice boards at least 3 days before the Jury.

. Written Examinations: Schools of Engineering, Management, Law, Agriculture and Psychology conduct written, time-bound, supervised examinations in designated halls. Key points: - Exam timetable shared via email, ERP portal or notice boards in advance - Seating plan displayed at venue entrance 30 minutes before each exam - Invigilators supervise the hall and maintain academic integrity - Paper Showing: after results, students may view assessed answer sheets (written exams only) on written request, within 5 working days of result declaration
This is read-only viewing — no marks changed during the session.

Grades and Result Classes:
Performance in each course is reported as a grade. Grades range from A+ (highest) down to F, with additional notations such as FR (Fail due to Ragging). SGPA and CGPA are calculated as credit-weighted averages.

CGPA Formula: CGPA = Σ(Ci × Si) / ΣCi
where Si = SGPA of the i-th semester, Ci = total credits in that semester.

Percentage Conversion: Percentage = CGPA × 10

Result Classes:
- First Class with Distinction
- First Class
- Second Class
- Pass Class
- Fail
(Exact CGPA cut-offs defined in the Academic Regulations)

Result Declaration and Fee Clearance:
Results are processed and declared after verification by the CoE and the School. If a student has outstanding fees at the time of result declaration, the result is withheld on the ERP portal until the Accounts department confirms full clearance. Clear dues well in advance of results.

Grievance & Re-evaluation:
Students may contest CCE or end-semester/Jury results through the formal grievance process. Submit application in writing. Re-evaluation decisions are final and no further appeals are entertained.

. Unfair Means (UFM): UFM includes copying, use of unauthorised material, and impersonation during an examination. If UFM is suspected: 1. Invigilator confiscates material, allows student to complete exam under observation 2. UFM report filed the same day 3. Case reviewed by CoE and referred to Disciplinary/UFM Committee 4. Student given opportunity to present case at hearing within 7 working days of report Outcomes may include cancellation of the paper, cancellation of the exam attempt, or suspension
All cases recorded and reported to Registrar and IQAC.
SECTION 4: SUPPLEMENTARY, BACKLOG, MAKEUP, IMPROVEMENT & SUMMER TERM EXAMS

If a student fails a course, misses an exam for an approved reason, or wishes to improve a grade, the following exam types are available. School Dean must verify eligibility and sign the application before it is processed. Examination fees apply.

Types of Special Exams:
- Supplementary/Backlog: for students who fail; CCE is done afresh in the special exam term (previous CCE does not carry forward)
- Makeup: for students who missed an exam for approved reasons
- Improvement: to improve grades; original CCE carries forward, only end-semester exam/Jury is re-attempted
- Summer Term: additional exam term offered in summer as applicable

ATKT (Allowed to Keep Terms):
ATKT is the policy defining the maximum number of backlogs a student may carry while progressing to the next year/semester. Backlog eligibility follows the ATKT rules in the Academic Regulations.

Conditions:
- Fees must be paid before application submitted; cash not accepted
- Cannot appear while fees outstanding or on academic probation/suspension (unless approved in writing by Dean and CoE)
- Application form available from Exam Office

Notification:
Exam Office notifies eligible students by email at least 30 days before the exam, with the application form, fee structure, application window and grievance process. Exam timetable shared at least 8 days before the first exam.
Avantika University offers internal scholarships to recognise and support deserving students on the basis of merit, financial need, or both. The Student Office runs the scholarship cycle.

. How it works: 1. Notification: Student Office emails all students announcing the scholarship at least 14 days before submission deadline, stating scholarship name, value, eligibility criteria, required documents, submission mode and contact for queries. 2. Application: Submit completed scholarship form in hard copy with required supporting documents before the deadline. Late submissions not accepted; any extension requires Registrar's approval. 3

Scrutiny: Applications reviewed for completeness, eligibility and valid documents within 4 working days of the deadline. Both shortlisted and non-shortlisted applicants informed by email within 2 working days. 4. Interview: A three-member panel (Student Office Dean/Manager and two faculty/senior members) evaluates shortlisted candidates against academic performance, financial need, supporting documents, and personal motivation using a standardised scoring rubric. 5
Selection & Disbursement: Final awardee list approved by Vice Chancellor, after which Finance department disburses scholarship to account or adjusts against fee dues.

The Student Office, with the Exam Section, issues academic transcripts and certificates. All clearances must be obtained before any document is released. Documents handed over only to the student or formally authorised person.

. Requesting a Transcript (before convocation): 1. Email transcript request to Student Office: name, enrolment number, programme, year, purpose, preferred collection mode (in person or by post) 2. Collect No-Dues Form from Student Office (issued within 1 working day) 3. Get No-Dues Form signed/stamped by all relevant departments — Accounts, Library, Hostel, etc. (each department signs within 2 working days; pending dues communicated for settlement) 4

Once clearances verified (within 2 working days of submission), request forwarded to Exam Section 5. Processing takes 5–7 working days for a digital copy and 2–3 weeks for a hard copy 6. Student notified when transcript ready (within 1 working day of receipt from Exam Section)

At or After Convocation:
For graduating batches, Student Office begins the No-Dues process at least 30 days before convocation; departments complete their part within 5 working days of receiving the batch list.

Collection and Authorisation:
- Collect in person
- Request postal dispatch (provide address; pay postage/courier in advance; office retains proof of dispatch)
- Authorise a third party: authorised person must bring original authorisation letter and copy of their photo ID. No document is handed to an unauthorised person under any circumstances.
SECTION 7: MENTORING, COUNSELLING & STUDENT WELLNESS
Every student is supported by a faculty mentor and has access to counselling and wellness resources.

Faculty Mentor System:
- Each mentor assigned 15–20 students (mentees) for the academic year
- Students informed of assigned mentor and schedule in the first week of the semester
- Mentors hold at least one formal session in the first week of every month
- Minimum 8 recorded sessions per academic year
- Open Office Hours: at least 1 hour per week for urgent concerns
- Mentors maintain a monthly log and submit a semester-end Action Taken Report
- Student Wellness Committee audits a random sample of logs every quarter

Red Flag Situations (requiring immediate escalation within 24 hours):
- Risk of self-harm
- Substance abuse
- Severe academic failure
- Serious disciplinary breach

In such cases, the mentor escalates within 24 hours so the student receives appropriate help.

Counselling:
Mentoring sessions are confidential and conversational, covering academic, personal and career matters. Campus counsellor and Health Centre also provide mental health support. Students showing distress are referred to the counsellor/psychologist within 24 hours. Support is available and confidential.

The University provides separate Boys and Girls hostels with a Warden responsible for welfare, safety and discipline of residents.

Eligibility:
- All students enrolled in a full-time programme may apply
- Priority given to outstation students
- Students with documented medical/special needs may apply under Special Category
- Seats subject to availability; allotment does not carry over — reapply each academic year
- Students with pending disciplinary action or outstanding dues may be denied admission

Room Allotment:
- Rooms allotted by Warden in consultation with Student Office, based on priority
- Room preference requests not entertained — rooms allotted at University's discretion
- Allotment confirmed only on receipt of hostel fee
- Room change: request after completing at least 30 days in allotted room, by submitting Room Change Request Form with valid reason

Mess Rules:
- Mess facility is mandatory for all hostel residents
- No non-vegetarian food allowed on campus

Check-in and Check-out:
- Bag and luggage check carried out at gate at every check-in
- Room Condition Inspection Form completed at check-in and check-out; new damage at check-out recovered from student
- At check-out: submit No-Dues Certificate and return all keys and hostel property

Security and Monitoring:
- CCTV operates 24×7 in common areas, corridors and entry/exit points (footage retained for at least 30 days)
- Security conducts night rounds every two hours between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM
- University is not liable for theft or damage to personal belongings

Outing and Day-pass Rules:
- During academic weekdays: Course Lead/Faculty Mentor signs outing pass
- On weekends and for evening outings: Warden signs and records in outing register
- Repeated late returns without intimation: first written warning, then suspension of outing, then parental meeting and escalation

Code of Conduct and Prohibited Items:
Prohibited substances (punishable offence, parents informed):
- Alcohol and restricted drinks
- Tobacco and smoking
- Any nuisance, fighting or quarrelling

Prohibited appliances (fire hazard, fine + seizure):
- Electric heaters, hot plates, electric irons, electric kettles, immersion rods

Internet: Wi-Fi provided as per IT policy; misuse leads to disconnection and disciplinary action.

Safety:
- Fire extinguishers at designated points; evacuation plan displayed on every floor
- At least one fire drill per semester
- Tampering with fire-safety equipment: immediate disciplinary action

Medical in Hostel:
- If ill: Warden arranges check-up with Campus Doctor and informs parents
- Communicable illness: may be moved to designated isolation room
- Emergency: Warden calls ambulance, informs parents and arranges hospital admission

Complaints:
- Minor complaints (maintenance, facilities): resolved within 3 working days
- Serious complaints (misconduct, safety): escalated within 24 hours

The campus Health Centre provides medical care to students and employees, with timely, safe and compassionate treatment.

Hours and Emergency Cover:
- OPD Hours: 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM daily, including weekends
- Duty nurse available on campus 24×7 for emergencies outside OPD hours
- Campus ambulance with driver available 24×7
- Emergency contact numbers posted at Health Centre, hostel notice boards, Warden's office, security post and on ERP portal

Services: - Consultation (OPD): examination and treatment by campus doctor/nurse; medication dispensed and recorded - Emergencies & referrals: patients triaged so most urgent seen first; referred to tie-up hospital when needed; referral process and parental notification handled by Health Centre and Warden - Communicable illness: isolation and notification protocols apply to protect others - Mental health: operates mental-health and counselling protocol; students showing distress referred within 24 hours - Confidentiality: medical information handled in confidence, consistent with patient rights
First-aid boxes maintained across campus and in hostels. Periodic health-awareness sessions and camps conducted.

Ragging in any form — physical, verbal, psychological or digital — is strictly prohibited at Avantika University and is a punishable offence under law and UGC regulations.

Digital ragging (through WhatsApp, Instagram, email or any social-media platform) is treated as seriously as physical ragging. This policy applies everywhere the University's name is involved — hostels, classrooms, transport, online platforms and off-campus activities.

Obligations and Protections:
- Every student (and parent/guardian) must submit an anti-ragging undertaking before the start of each academic year; no student officially enrolled without it
- University runs awareness sessions for freshers and seniors during orientation
- Anti-ragging information displayed across campus
- Surprise inspections at least twice a month through the Anti-Ragging Squad

How to Report Ragging:
- UGC National Anti-Ragging Helpline: 1800-180-5522 (available at all times, 24x7)
- Contact the Anti-Ragging Cell/Committee directly
- Anonymous complaints accepted, including via drop box
- Identity of complainants protected to prevent retaliation
- Anti-Ragging Cell acknowledges every complaint within 24 hours
- Dedicated 24×7 contact number posted on website and hostel notice boards

Investigation and Action Timeline:
- Investigation begins within 24 hours of complaint
- Anti-Ragging Squad submits findings within 3 working days
- Committee meets within 5 working days
- Decision issued within 3 working days of the hearing
- Accused given opportunity to present their case before any action taken
- During investigation, victim is counselled and kept safe

Penalties may include: warning, suspension, dismissal, or legal action as per UGC regulations. Guilty findings communicated in writing to student and parents; VC notified.
FR Grade: Students found guilty of ragging are awarded FR (Fail due to Ragging) grade in all courses that semester.

The University provides a bus service for students and staff, run by the Transport Coordinator/Officer.

Eligibility and Registration:
- All full-time students may register via ERP portal or Transport/Student Office
- Transport fees must be paid before availing the service; no refund after semester begins
- Registration closes at least 5 working days before semester starts
- Late requests need Transport Officer approval
- Must carry Transport ID / fee card / valid University ID when boarding; students without valid ID may be denied entry

Routes and Safety:
- Routes and timings finalised based on boarding points and published at least 3 days before service begins
- Mid-semester changes communicated at least 24 hours in advance via SMS/email/WhatsApp
- Board only allotted bus at designated stop
- Movement in a moving bus is not allowed
- Misconduct or damage to property: disciplinary action
- Emergency contact numbers (Transport Coordinator, Security, Health Centre, 108 ambulance) displayed inside each vehicle
- Backup vehicle dispatched within 30 minutes in case of breakdown

Use the right channel for different issues: - Academic queries & course issues: Faculty → Course Lead → School Dean - Examination results/issues: Exam Office / Controller of Examinations (CoE) - Hostel and residential issues: Hostel Warden → Student Office - Health and medical issues: Health Centre (24×7 emergency cover) - Anti-ragging: Anti-Ragging Cell / UGC Helpline: 1800-180-5522 (24×7) - Scholarship/financial: Student Office, then Finance Department - Transcripts & certificates: Student Office, then Exam Section - Transport issues: Transport Office - General student welfare: Student Office / Faculty Mentor
Most issues resolved at the first level. Escalate only if not resolved.

- UGC National Anti-Ragging Helpline: 1800-180-5522 (24×7, free)
- Specific phone numbers and names of current office-holders are published on the University website, ERP portal and notice boards at the start of each academic year
- Campus Ambulance: available 24×7 (number posted at Health Centre, hostels, security post, ERP portal)
- Health Centre OPD: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM daily
- Duty Nurse: 24×7 for emergencies

Offices: - Student Office: enrolment, registration, scholarships, transcripts, hostel, overall student welfare - Exam Office (Controller of Examinations): examinations, results, re-evaluation, UFM - Hostel Warden (Boys/Girls): residential life, room allotment, outing passes, hostel discipline - Transport Office: bus registration, routes, timings - Health Centre: medical consultation, emergency, counselling referrals - Accounts Department: fee clearance, scholarships disbursement - Library: no-dues for transcripts - Faculty Mentor: first point of contact for academic and personal matters

Common forms available from the relevant office, Student Office, or University portal: - Enrolment Form and document checklist - Acknowledgement Report (enrolment) - Hostel Admission Form - Hostel Undertaking and Anti-Ragging Undertaking - Room Condition Inspection Form - Room Change Request Form - No-Dues Certificate - Scholarship application form - Transcript request and No-Dues Form - Authorisation letter for third-party document collection - Supplementary/Backlog/Makeup/Improvement application form - Withdrawal Form - Exam grievance form - Transport registration / fee card

Disclaimer: This handbook is a consolidated summary from official SOPs. It does not replace those SOPs, Academic Regulations, UGC norms, or official notifications, which remain the authoritative sources.

AVANTIKA UNIVERSITY - OFFICIAL WEBSITE INFORMATION
Source: www.avantikauniversity.edu.in
Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh | An MIT Group Institution

Avantika University is India's first design-centered university, established in 2017 through an Act of the Madhya Pradesh State Legislature. It is a state-private institution and part of the MIT Group, which operates 5 universities and 80+ institutions catering to over 70,000 students.

The university operates under the motto: "Education for the Next Generation to Build a Better World" — blending Art, Design, Innovation, Creativity, and Enterprise into an interdisciplinary curriculum with experiential learning.

Key Facts:
- Established: 2017
- Location: Vishwanathpuram, Lekoda Village, Ujjain – 456006, Madhya Pradesh, India
- Campus Size: 60 acres
- Part of: MIT Group of Institutions
- Type: State-Private University
- Distance from Indore Airport: 58 km
- Distance from Nanakheda Bus Stand: 30 minutes (regular shuttle available)
- UGC Approved
- NIRF Ranked
- Registered with MPPURC (Madhya Pradesh Private University Regulatory Commission)
- MOE (Ministry of Education) recognized
- NAD (National Accreditation Database) registered
